text: fat is 49.2g. omega3 is where the section starts to wobble. for nuts, fat quality is supposed to be the main advantage.
carbs is 16.1g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.
protein is 25.8g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 56% DV, vitamin b12 at 0% DV stand out most. that helps, but vitamins still are not the main selling point.
magnesium at 42% DV, zinc at 28% DV, iron at 25% DV stand out most. that is useful, but still not a huge mineral edge.

on the plus side. unusually protein-forward for a nut because tests whether nuts can handle an unusually high-protein member. cheap and practical because real-world advantage. strong satiety because useful practical upside.

the drawbacks next. allergy risk matters because meaningful downside. omega-3 support is weak because a big test of the current nuts philosophy. awkward category identity because botanically a legume, culturally a nut.
Peanuts is B tier. Best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
